data.title: Garmin raises dividend by 16.7% to $1.05
data.description:
• Garmin (GRMN) declares $1.05/share monthly dividend, 16.7% increase from prior dividend of $0.90.
  • Forward yield 5.31%
  • Payable June 26; for shareholders of record June 15; ex-div June 15.
  • Payable Sept 25; for shareholders of record Sept 11; ex-div Sept 11.
  • Payable Dec 24; for shareholders of record Dec 11; ex-div Dec 11.
  • Payable March 26; for shareholders of record March 12; ex-div March 12.
  • See GRMN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Compass Pathways prices $150M ADS offering
data.description:
• Compass Pathways (CMPS) priced its public offering of 17.5M ADSs at $8.00 per ADS. It also offered pre-funded warrants to purchase up to 1.25M ADSs at $7.9999 each to certain institutional investors, with underwriters granted a 30-day option for an additional 2.8125M ADSs.
  • The gross proceeds are expected to total $150M, with closing anticipated on or about February 20, 2026. 
  • The net proceeds will fund ongoing Phase 3 trials COMP005 and COMP006 (likely for treatment-resistant depression), a Phase 2b/3 trial of COMP360 psilocybin for PTSD, commercial readiness acceleration, and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Wyndham Hotels & Resorts beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Wyndham Hotels & Resorts press release (WH): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.93 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $334M (-2.1% Y/Y) beats by $7.28M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA decreased 2% to $165 million compared to $168 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.

  • The Company generated $367 million of net cash provided by operating activities and $433 million of adjusted free cash flow in 2025. The Company ended the quarter with a cash balance of $64 million and $840 million in total liquidity. 

  • The Company's net debt leverage ratio was 3.5 times at December 31, 2025, at the midpoint of the Company's 3 to 4 times stated target range and in line with expectations.

  • The Company's Board of Directors recently authorized a 5% increase in the quarterly cash dividend to $0.43 per share, beginning with the dividend expected to be declared in first quarter 2026.

  • Outlook

    The Company provided the following outlook for full-year 2026: 

       

    2026 Outlook

    		  

    Year-over-year rooms growth (a)

    		  

    4.0% - 4.5%

    		  

    Year-over-year global RevPAR growth (b)

    		  

    (1.5%) - 0.5%

    		  

    Fee-related and other revenues

    		  

    $1.46 - $1.49 billion vs. consensus of $1.51B

    		  

    Adjusted EBITDA (c)

    		  

    $730 - $745 million

    		  

    Adjusted net income

    		  

    $354 - $368 million

    		  

    Adjusted diluted EPS

    		  

    $4.62 - $4.80 vs. consensus of $4.89

    		  

    Free cash flow conversion rate

    		  

    55% - 60%

data.title: Genesis Energy prices upsized $750M senior notes
data.description:
• Genesis Energy (GEL) priced a public offering of $750M 6.75% senior notes due 2034. The offering was upsized from the previously announced $500M in aggregate principal.
  • The notes are priced at 100% of principal and co-issued with subsidiary Genesis Energy Finance Corporation, guaranteed by most subsidiaries except unrestricted ones.
  • The net proceeds will redeem all outstanding 7.75% senior notes due 2028 and support general purposes, including repaying revolving borrowings under the senior secured credit facility.
  • The offering of the notes is expected to settle on March 4, 2026

data.title: Manhattan judge bars ex-Palantir employees from recruiting staff for Percepta AI
data.description:
A federal judge in Manhattan on Wednesday approved Palantir Technologies’ (PLTR) request to bar two former employees from recruiting its staff to join the company they started last year, Percepta AI. However, the judge declined to prohibit the ex-employees from continuing to work at their new venture.

The ruling by U.S. District Judge Paul Oetken will be in place pending the outcome of Palantir's lawsuit filed in October.

Palantir (PLTR) sued Percepta co-founder and CEO Hirsh Jain last year, claiming he “began an aggressive campaign to recruit numerous Palantir employees” after leaving the company in August 2024. 

Oetken also barred Joanna Cohen, a former Palantir engineer now with Percepta, from breaching a confidentiality agreement she had signed with Palantir, according to media reports.

But the judge denied Palantir's request to block the defendants from violating agreements they signed not to compete with the company or solicit its customers, which would have barred them from working at Percepta indefinitely.

Percepta is owned by venture capital firm General Catalyst and was publicly unveiled in October.

data.title: Materialise GAAP EPS of $0.12, revenue of $82.44M beats by $0.42M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Materialise press release (MTLS): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.12.
  • Revenue of $82.44M beats by $0.42M, increased by 6.8% Y/Y.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased to 9,524 kEUR for the fourth quarter of 2025 from 4,306 kEUR for the 2024 period. Adjusted EBIT amounted to 3,980 kEUR, compared to (1,195) kEUR for the same period in 2024.
  • Cash flow from operating activities for the fourth quarter of the year 2025 was 5,274 kEUR, compared to 6,218 kEUR for the same period in 2024.  
  • Total capital expenditures for the fourth quarter of the year 2025 amounted to 4,411 kEUR.
  • The generated free cash flow over the fourth quarter of 2025 amounted to 4,511 kEUR.
  • Outlook: "On a consolidated level, we therefore expect our full year revenues for 2026 to grow to a range of 273,000 to 283,000 kEUR. We will continue investing in our Materialise Medical and Software segments while maintaining disciplined cost control and optimization, in particular in our Materialise Manufacturing segment and in our overhead. As a result, we expect our Adjusted EBIT to reach 10,000 to 12,000 kEUR for fiscal year 2026.”

data.title: Aegon reports FY results
data.description:
• Aegon press release (AEG): Full-year 2025 net result of EUR 980 million, up 45% compared with 2024.
  • Full-year 2025 operating result of EUR 1.7 billion, up 15% compared with 2024.
  • fFull-year 2025 OCG of EUR 1.3 billion, Aegon exceeds its target of EUR 1.2 billion.
  • Free cash flow of EUR 388 million, contributes to full-year free cash flow of EUR 829 million, consistent with the target of around EUR 800 million.
  • 2025 final dividend of EUR 0.21 per common share proposed, an increase of 11% compared with 2024 final dividend, enabling Aegon to meet its EUR 0.40 target for full-year 2025.
